To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きのみやしんしや いすしはつま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'静岡県伊豆市八幡106', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県賀茂郡河津町田中', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929624}} == Japanese content == {{混同|link1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きのみやしんしや いすしはつま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'静岡県伊豆市八幡106', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県賀茂郡河津町田中', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929624}} == Japanese content == {{混同|link1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きのみやしんしや いすしはつま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{'text': '静岡県伊豆市八幡106', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県賀茂郡河津町田中', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929624}} == Japanese content == {{混同|link1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きのみやしんしや いすしはつま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * スキホコ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == postal code (P281) == * 410-2505https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県伊豆市八幡106', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県賀茂郡河津町田中', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929624}} == Japanese content == {{混同|link1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa|1=Nanboku-chō period}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169|1=Nakaizu, Shizuoka}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine (Izu)}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きのみやしんしや いすしはつま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * スキホコ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == postal code (P281) == * 410-2505https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県伊豆市八幡106', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県賀茂郡河津町田中', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929624}} == Japanese content == {{混同|link1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa|1=Nanboku-chō period}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169|1=Nakaizu, Shizuoka}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine (Izu)}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きのみやしんしや いすしはつま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県伊豆市八幡106', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県賀茂郡河津町田中', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929624}} == Japanese content == {{混同|link1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa|1=Nanboku-chō period}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169|1=Nakaizu, Shizuoka}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine (Izu)}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きのみやしんしや いすしはつま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}[[Category:qq]]

Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa|1=Nanboku-chō period}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169|1=Nakaizu, Shizuoka}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine (Izu)}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きのみやしんしや いすしはつま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)}}[[Category:qq]]

Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa|1=Nanboku-chō period}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169|1=Nakaizu, Shizuoka}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine (Izu)}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa|1=Nanboku-chō period}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169|1=Nakaizu, Shizuoka}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine (Izu)}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa|1=Nanboku-chō period}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169|1=Nakaizu, Shizuoka}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine (Izu)}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県伊豆市八幡106', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県賀茂郡河津町田中', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929624}} == Japanese content == {{混同|link1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa|1=Nanboku-chō period}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169|1=Nakaizu, Shizuoka}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine (Izu)}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県伊豆市八幡106', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県賀茂郡河津町田中', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929624}} == Japanese content == {{混同|link1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa|1=Nanboku-chō period}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169|1=Nakaizu, Shizuoka}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine (Izu)}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県伊豆市八幡106', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県賀茂郡河津町田中', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929624}} == Japanese content == {{混同|link1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Jōwa|1=Nanboku-chō period}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|WD=Q6960169|1=Nakaizu, Shizuoka}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|WD=Q11390944|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|WD=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|WD=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine (Izu)}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県伊豆市八幡106', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県賀茂郡河津町田中', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929624}} == Japanese content == {{混同|link1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|qid=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|qid=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|qid=Q264012|lt=Jōwa|1=Nanboku-chō period}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|qid=Q6960169|1=Nakaizu, Shizuoka}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|qid=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|qid=Q11390944|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|qid=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|qid=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|qid=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|qid=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|qid=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine (Izu)}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|qid=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]

Kinomiya Shrine|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine (はちまんぐう きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |名称 = Kinomiya Shrine
(Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=Izu City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|qid=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 106[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402199 来宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |位置 = {{coord|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = around October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kinomiya Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|26.1|N|139|0|8.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|qid=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. To distinguish it from other Kinomiya Shrines, it is also called '''Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine''' (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000109.html City-designated Natural Monuments] - Izu City. === Overview === The shrine buildings, which had fallen into ruin, are said to have been rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|qid=Q264012|lt=Jōwa|1=Nanboku-chō period}} era (1345-1349), and the shrine came to serve as the general tutelary shrine of the 16 Ōmi villages (most of the former {{ill|lt=Nakaizu Town|Q6960169|qid=Q6960169|1=Nakaizu, Shizuoka}} area)Precinct history board. The pattern of a “Kinomiya Shrine” as part of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya worship|Q11296898|qid=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} being brought into a place named “Hachiman” is similar to nearby {{ill|lt=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|Q11390944|qid=Q11390944|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|qid=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}. Also, in works such as ''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dadai|Q11588823|qid=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}'', it is named, along with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu|Q1203861|qid=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, as a candidate shrine for “Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the {{ill|lt=list of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|qid=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}. (In the northwestern nearby area, Kami-Shiraiwa, there is also {{ill|lt=Ōmiya Shrine (Izu)|Q113679907|qid=Q113679907|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine (Izu)}}, a candidate shrine for another Shikinaisha, “Ōasa Shrine.”) It has a distinctive landscape in which a torii and approach are formed between road and rice fields, and at the entrance to the precincts stand giant cedars designated as city natural monuments. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === * Festival float (city-designated cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail000155.html City-designated crafts] - Izu City) * Torii cedars of Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (city-designated natural monument) === Festivals === * Annual grand festival (around October 19) === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|qid=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (city-commissioned self-operated route[https://www.at-s.com/news/article/shizuoka/932289.html Route bus Nakaizu Line to be city-operated] - Shizuoka Shimbun 2021/7/21): 3-5 minutes on foot northeast from “Nakazato” or “Hatsuma” bus stops. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]
